name: Hormonal acne
dek: Adult acne, jawline-clustered, premenstrually predictable, persistent past the years anyone told you it would be. Treated topically the same way as teen acne — but the third lever is systemic. The patients who do not improve on a topical-only protocol almost always benefit from a derm consult about spironolactone or COCPs.
oneliner: Jawline, premenstrual, persistent. Topical + sometimes systemic.

triggers.n: Distinguishes from comedonal teen acne, which favours the T-zone.
Days 18–28 of the cycle. Predictable, hormonal.
Adult-onset or persistent — the defining feature.
Hirsutism, irregular cycles. Worth a workup if present.

ingredients.name: Benzoyl peroxide 2.5%
ingredients.evidence: Reference for inflammatory papules · over decades

ingredients.name: Salicylic acid 2%
ingredients.evidence: Mild adjunct · not a primary treatment

protocolAm: Lightweight moisturiser (CeraVe AM, Toleriane Double Repair)
protocolPm: Cleansing oil → gel cleanser (double-cleanse if wearing SPF + makeup)
Spot-treatment: BPO 2.5% on inflammatory lesions only
